name: Chhole Alu
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Potatoes, boiled, peeled and cubed
2 large Onions, cut into thin rings
A few Mint leaves, finely sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the channa in water overnight.
2. The next morning, boil the soaked channa with a pinch of soda and salt until tender and dry.
3. Heat 4 tablespoons ghee in a pan. Add ginger and onions; fry until the mixture turns limp and turns an almond color.
4. Add tomatoes and spices (cumin seeds, coriander powder, chili powder, cardamom) to the pan. Cook until the ghee separates from the oil.
5. Mash with a wooden spoon into a smooth paste, then add 1 cup water and powdered jaggery. Stir well and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and add the cooked channa, slit green chillies, and cubed potatoes. Cook until the chillies are done.
7. Meanwhile, soak tamarind in 4 tablespoons of water until soft. Squeeze out the pulp and stir into the chana mixture.
8. Mix well and continue cooking until the gravy thickens considerably.
9. Turn off heat. Garnish with onion rings, minced green chillies, coriander leaves, mint slices, and lime wedges before serving.
